content: The prefrontal-hippocampal interaction mechanism during human memory retrieval can be used to redesign the memory gating and selection mechanism of 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents to overcome their working memory capacity bottlenecks during long-horizon episodic planning. This adaptation would allow active learning agents to dynamically prioritize high-relevance retrieved episodic memories for current reasoning tasks, rather than storing all unfiltered encountered experiences.
